In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8qqc00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.913qq97!4d139.9691285!16s/g/qqf9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{温泉}}{{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8qqc00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.913qq97!4d139.9691285!16s/g/qqf9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{温泉}}{{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8qqc00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.913qq97!4d139.9691285!16s/g/qqf9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{温泉}}{{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8qqc00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.913qq97!4d139.9691285!16s/g/qqf9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.913qq97!4d139.9691285!16s/g/qqf9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{温泉}}{{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{温泉}}{{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}} {{Onsen}} [[Category:New]]

Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}} {{Onsen}} [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983年}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}} {{Onsen}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}} {{Onsen}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year }}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year }}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}} {{Onsen}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Onsen}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 6]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year }}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year }}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year }} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Onsen}}

It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|ja|1983rd year }}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year }}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year }} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}}

It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛|12=draft}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛|12=draft}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩|12=draft}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=draft}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属|12=draft}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位|12=draft}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|12=draft}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring|12=draft}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town|12=draft}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=draft}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=draft}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=draft}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}}

It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛|12=draft}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛|12=draft}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩|12=draft}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=draft}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属|12=draft}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位|12=draft}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|12=draft}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot spring|12=draft}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town|12=draft}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishinasuno Station|ja|西那須野駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|ja|那須塩原駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=draft}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=draft}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot spring areas in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=draft}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hot spring areas in Japan]] [[Category:Requests for images in Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Geography of Nasu-Shiobara]] [[Category:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:温泉街]] [[Category:Moor hot spring]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Nishinasuno Onsen]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[en:Nishinasuno Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinasuno Onsen}}

{{infobox Onsen |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen

The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC|12=draft}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|12=draft}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |出典 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=draft}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nasu-Shiobara|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|12=draft}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|muscle pain|ja|筋肉痛|12=draft}}, {{ill|joint pain|ja|関節痛|12=draft}}, {{ill|frozen shoulder|ja|五十肩|12=draft}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=draft}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|heavy metals|ja|重金属|12=draft}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|oxidation-reduction potential|ja|酸化還元電位|12=draft}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|12=draft}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|spa town|lt=onsen town|ja|温泉街|1=spa town|lt=Onsen town|12=draft}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above.
