Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishinasuno Station]] or [[:en:Nasushiobara Station|Nasushiobara Station]], take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] }} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q134696315]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]] {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |References = |External links = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasu-Shiobara]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in [[:en:1983|1983]]The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bato Onsen|1=Bato Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668118}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In [[:en:1994|1994]], the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for [[:en:Myalgia|muscle pain]], [[:en:Arthralgia|joint pain]], [[:en:Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|frozen shoulder]], {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paralysis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麻痺|1=paralysis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107231}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of [[:en:Heavy metals|heavy metals]] and its exceptionally low [[:en:Reduction potential|oxidation-reduction potential]], one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in [[:en:2018|2018]] alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]] consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishinasuno Station]] or [[:en:Nasushiobara Station|Nasushiobara Station]], take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] }} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in [[:en:1983|1983]]The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bato Onsen|1=Bato Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668118}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In [[:en:1994|1994]], the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for [[:en:Myalgia|muscle pain]], [[:en:Arthralgia|joint pain]], [[:en:Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|frozen shoulder]], {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paralysis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麻痺|1=paralysis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107231}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of [[:en:Heavy metals|heavy metals]] and its exceptionally low [[:en:Reduction potential|oxidation-reduction potential]], one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in [[:en:2018|2018]] alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]] consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishinasuno Station]] or [[:en:Nasushiobara Station|Nasushiobara Station]], take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] }} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]] {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |References = |External links = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Nasushiobara|Nasu-Shiobara]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in [[:en:1983|1983]]The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bato Onsen|1=Bato Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668118}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In [[:en:1994|1994]], the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for [[:en:Myalgia|muscle pain]], [[:en:Arthralgia|joint pain]], [[:en:Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|frozen shoulder]], {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paralysis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麻痺|1=paralysis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107231}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of [[:en:Heavy metals|heavy metals]] and its exceptionally low [[:en:Reduction potential|oxidation-reduction potential]], one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in [[:en:2018|2018]] alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]] consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishinasuno Station]] or [[:en:Nasushiobara Station|Nasushiobara Station]], take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The spring is notable for the absence of [[:en:Heavy metals|heavy metals]] and its exceptionally low [[:en:Reduction potential|oxidation-reduction potential]], one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in [[:en:2018|2018]] alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The [[:en:Spa town|Onsen town]] consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From [[:en:Nishi-Nasuno Station|Nishinasuno Station]] or [[:en:Nasushiobara Station|Nasushiobara Station]], take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q134696315]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |References = |External links = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasu-Shiobara}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bato Onsen|1=Bato Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668118}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|Myalgia|en|Myalgia|WD=Q474959|lt=muscle pain}}, {{ill|Arthralgia|en|Arthralgia|WD=Q683498|lt=joint pain}}, {{ill|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|en|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|WD=Q52854|lt=frozen shoulder}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paralysis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麻痺|1=paralysis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107231}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|Heavy metals|en|Heavy metals|WD=Q105789|lt=heavy metals}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|Reduction potential|en|Reduction potential|WD=Q186487|lt=oxidation-reduction potential}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|WD=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishinasuno Station}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|en|Nasushiobara Station|WD=Q369523|lt=Nasushiobara Station}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |References = |External links = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasu-Shiobara}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bato Onsen|1=Bato Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668118}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|Myalgia|en|Myalgia|WD=Q474959|lt=muscle pain}}, {{ill|Arthralgia|en|Arthralgia|WD=Q683498|lt=joint pain}}, {{ill|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|en|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|WD=Q52854|lt=frozen shoulder}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paralysis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麻痺|1=paralysis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107231}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|Heavy metals|en|Heavy metals|WD=Q105789|lt=heavy metals}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|Reduction potential|en|Reduction potential|WD=Q186487|lt=oxidation-reduction potential}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|WD=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishinasuno Station}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|en|Nasushiobara Station|WD=Q369523|lt=Nasushiobara Station}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|Myalgia|en|Myalgia|WD=Q474959|lt=muscle pain}}, {{ill|Arthralgia|en|Arthralgia|WD=Q683498|lt=joint pain}}, {{ill|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|en|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|WD=Q52854|lt=frozen shoulder}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paralysis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麻痺|1=paralysis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107231}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|Heavy metals|en|Heavy metals|WD=Q105789|lt=heavy metals}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|Reduction potential|en|Reduction potential|WD=Q186487|lt=oxidation-reduction potential}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|WD=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishinasuno Station}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|en|Nasushiobara Station|WD=Q369523|lt=Nasushiobara Station}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |References = |External links = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasu-Shiobara|1=Nasushiobara}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983|1=1983}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bato Onsen|1=Q11668118|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668118}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|Myalgia|en|Myalgia|WD=Q474959|lt=muscle pain|1=Myalgia}}, {{ill|Arthralgia|en|Arthralgia|WD=Q683498|lt=joint pain|1=Arthralgia}}, {{ill|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|en|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|WD=Q52854|lt=frozen shoulder|1=Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paralysis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麻痺|1=Paralysis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107231}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|Heavy metals|en|Heavy metals|WD=Q105789|lt=heavy metals|1=Heavy metals}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|Reduction potential|en|Reduction potential|WD=Q186487|lt=oxidation-reduction potential|1=Reduction potential}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|WD=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town|1=Spa town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishinasuno Station|1=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|en|Nasushiobara Station|WD=Q369523|lt=Nasushiobara Station|1=Nasushiobara Station}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan|1=List of hot springs in Japan}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|WD=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town|1=Spa town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishinasuno Station|1=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|en|Nasushiobara Station|WD=Q369523|lt=Nasushiobara Station|1=Nasushiobara Station}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan|1=List of hot springs in Japan}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{infobox Onsen |名称 = Nishinasuno Onsen
NISHINASUNO ONSEN |image = CKT20221-C15-10.jpg |コメント = Aerial photo of the surrounding area. The central upper part is Ōtaka no Yu, and the central lower-left part is the Marronnier Hotel. |所在地 = Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 548-350 (Ōtaka no Yu) Tochigi Prefecture, Nasu-Shiobara City, Iguchi 546-1 (Mori no Yu) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 58|経度秒 = 6 |交通 = {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} {{ill|Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja|西那須野塩原IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinasuno-Shiobara IC|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西那須野塩原インターチェンジ|1=Nishinasuno-shiobara interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629575}}, 3.2 km by road |泉質 = Ōtaka no Yu: Sodium chloride bicarbonate spring ({{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}}), {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}
Mori no Yu: {{ill|Simple spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} |泉温 = Approximately 40°C (both are high-temperature springs) |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = Both weakly alkaline |浸透圧の分類 = Both hypotonic |温泉施設数 = |宿泊施設数 = 2 |年間浴客数 = |統計年 = |References = |External links = |特記事項 = Not part of {{ill|Nasu Onsen|ja|那須温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Onsen|1=Nasu Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642937}} or {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}. }} '''Nishinasuno Onsen''' (西那須野温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Nasushiobara|en|Nasushiobara|WD=Q498027|lt=Nasu-Shiobara|1=Nasushiobara}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is situated 3.7 km northwest of Nishinasuno Station. Two sources, '''Ōtaka no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nishinasu Onsen - History of Japanese Onsen |url=https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/,%20https://www.onsen-history.com/1713/ |website=www.onsen-history.com |date=2019-08-30 |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja |last=admin}} and '''Mori no Yu'''{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel Official Site |url=https://nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp/ |website=nasu-marronnier-hotel.jp |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}, are used. == Overview == === Ōtaka no Yu === Ōtaka no Yu is a relatively new hot spring developed in {{ill|1983|en|1983|WD=Q2434|lt=1983|1=1983}}The Nasu region has been famous for hot springs for a long time, but major hot spring areas such as {{ill|Shiobara Onsen|ja|塩原温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Onsen|1=Shiobara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428634}}, {{ill|Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja|那須湯本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu-Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Bato Onsen|ja|馬頭温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bato Onsen|1=Q11668118|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668118}} are all inconveniently located far from major stations or interchanges.. In {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}, the owner opened a lodging facility named "Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Retreat Center"{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu Jōmon Ōtaka Retreat Center |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF+%E7%B8%84%E6%96%87%E5%A4%A7%E9%B7%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%83%88%E3%82%BB%E3%83%B3%E3%82%BF%E3%83%BC/@36.9146184,139.9636437,1653m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f811c00000000:0x98bb776450e72050!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9158575!4d139.9704627!16s/g/1vg_7d6g?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is effective for {{ill|Myalgia|en|Myalgia|WD=Q474959|lt=muscle pain|1=Myalgia}}, {{ill|Arthralgia|en|Arthralgia|WD=Q683498|lt=joint pain|1=Arthralgia}}, {{ill|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|en|Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder|WD=Q52854|lt=frozen shoulder|1=Adhesive capsulitis of the shoulder}}, {{ill|paralysis|ja|運動麻痺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paralysis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 麻痺|1=Paralysis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q107231}}, and other conditions{{Cite web |title=Ōtaka no Yu |url=https://www.ootakanoyu.com/ryokan/ |website=Ōtaka no Yu |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja}}. The spring is notable for the absence of {{ill|Heavy metals|en|Heavy metals|WD=Q105789|lt=heavy metals|1=Heavy metals}} and its exceptionally low {{ill|Reduction potential|en|Reduction potential|WD=Q186487|lt=oxidation-reduction potential|1=Reduction potential}}, one of the lowest in Japan{{Cite web |title=Encountering Ancient Somatid Hot Springs |url=https://tenkataihei.com/?p=4878 |website=Tenkataihei |access-date=2024-06-24}}. === Mori no Yu === Mori no Yu was developed in {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}} alongside the opening of the Nasu Marronnier Hotel{{Cite web |title=Nasu Marronnier Hotel |url=https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E9%82%A3%E9%A0%88%E3%83%9E%E3%83%AD%E3%83%8B%E3%82%A8%E3%83%9B%E3%83%86%E3%83%AB/@36.9129538,139.9684755,1656m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m9!3m8!1s0x601f8165a946692d:0x98cc5deba1564e01!5m2!4m1!1i2!8m2!3d36.9131197!4d139.9691285!16s/g/11f9yhwv_9?entry=ttu |website=Google Maps |access-date=2024-06-24 |language=ja-US}}. It is a rare {{ill|moor spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} in Japan, earning the nickname "Bijin no Yu" (The Spring of Beauty) for its high bicarbonate ion content. It is also effective for the same conditions as Ōtaka no Yu. == Facilities == The {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|WD=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town|1=Spa town}} consists of only the two lodging facilities mentioned above. Day-use bathing is available at Ōtaka no Yu for ¥800 (weekdays) and at Marronnier Hotel for ¥600. == Access == From {{ill|Nishi-Nasuno Station|en|Nishi-Nasuno Station|WD=Q872877|lt=Nishinasuno Station|1=Nishi-Nasuno Station}} or {{ill|Nasushiobara Station|en|Nasushiobara Station|WD=Q369523|lt=Nasushiobara Station|1=Nasushiobara Station}}, take the {{ill|Yū-Bus|ja|那須塩原市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yū-Bus|1=Nasushiobara City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642911}} Nishinasuno Line to "Iguchi-kita" (Ōtaka no Yu) or "{{ill|International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja|国際医療福祉大学病院前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/International University of Health and Welfare Hospital|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (Marronnier Hotel){{Cite web |title=Bus Routes - Nationwide Bus Stop & Route Map |url=https://bus-routes.net/ |website=bus-routes.net |access-date=2024-06-24}}. == Notes == === Note === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan|1=List of hot springs in Japan}} * {{ill|Senbonmatsu Ranch|ja|千本松牧場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbonmatsu Ranch|1=Senbonmatsu Farm|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405592}} (nearby hot spring facility) {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|西那須野温泉|version=104592540|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:西那須野温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q134696315}} [[Category:Geography of Nasushiobara, Tochigi]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Image requests in Nasushiobara City]] [[Category:Moor hot springs]] [[Category:Spa towns in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
